cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, except in leap years when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year, right? Leap years are divisible by 4, but 2001 divided by 4 is 500.25, so no. So Jane was born on 2/28/2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So we need to figure out what today's date is. Since she was born on 2/28/2001, her 16th birthday would be 16 years later. Let me add 16 to 2001: 2001 + 16 = 2017. Wait, but wait—is today 2/28/2017? But wait, if today is her 16th birthday, then today is 2/28/2017. Because each birthday is the anniversary of your birth date. So today is 2/28/2017. Now the question is asking: What is the date one year ago from today? So if today is 2/28/2017, then one year ago would be 2/28/2016. But wait, 2016 was a leap year, right? Because 2016 divided by 4 is 504, no remainder. So February 2016 had 29 days. But does that affect anything? Well, when you subtract a year from a date, you just subtract 1 from the year, unless the original date is February 29, which would make it February 28 in a non-leap year. But in this case, the original date is February 28. So even if the following year is a leap year, subtracting a year from February 28, 2017 would take us to February 28, 2016. Because February 28 exists in both leap and non-leap years. So the answer should be 02/28/2016. Let me check the options. Option C is 02/28/2016. So that's probably the correct answer. Let me double-check. Alternatively, maybe there's a trick. Suppose today is her 16th birthday, which is February 28, 2017. One year ago from today would be February 28, 2016. But let me check if there's any possibility that the problem is considering that in some contexts, a year ago might be considered as 365 days ago. But in date calculations, when someone says "one year ago," it's typically the same day and month, just minus one year, unless that date doesn't exist (like if you have March 1, 2017 and subtract a year when 2016 is a leap year). But in this case, since the date is February 28, which exists in both leap and non-leap years, it's straightforward. So 2/28/2017 minus one year is 2/28/2016.
